skill.name: paypal-integration
skill.description: Guides PayPal checkout, subscriptions, IPN verification, refunds, and sandbox testing for payment integrations.
skill.summary: Build and review PayPal payment flows for checkout, recurring billing, notifications, and refunds.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution and weak-crypto alerts are false positives from Markdown fences and payment-related wording. The network findings are true positives because the guide includes PayPal API and IPN request examples, but they align with the skill purpose and do not show exfiltration or prompt injection. Risk remains medium because the examples handle financial transactions and require production validation, timeout handling, and credential controls.

medium_findings.title: PayPal API Network Calls in Sample Code
medium_findings.description: The network findings are true positives. The skill shows requests to PayPal SDK, OAuth, checkout, subscriptions, IPN, and refund endpoints. This is expected for a PayPal integration guide, but generated code can move money or expose credentials if users apply it without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly imports HTTP libraries and calls PayPal endpoints. The endpoints match the skill purpose, so this is a legitimate but security-sensitive network capability.

medium_findings.title: Payment Notification Handling Needs Production Validation
medium_findings.description: The IPN example verifies messages with PayPal, but the surrounding processing does not show receiver, amount, currency, order ownership, or full transaction state checks. This can lead to business logic errors if copied into production without additional validation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The example clearly processes completed, refunded, and reversed payments after PayPal echo verification. No evidence of malicious intent exists, but important production authorization checks are absent from the displayed flow.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misidentified as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The cited backticks open and close Markdown code examples for JavaScript and Python, not Ruby backtick execution or shell invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited location is a Markdown fence delimiter around static examples. No shell command execution syntax is present at those locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Is a Textual False Positive
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm finding at the description line is a false positive. The line describes PayPal payment processing and contains no cryptographic algorithm or implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is frontmatter text about payment processing. I found no evidence of weak cryptographic code at that location.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Data Field False Positives
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are false positives. The cited lines store PayPal credentials passed into a client or read transaction fields from notification data; they do not inspect the host system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited variables are PayPal client and transaction identifiers in sample code. No host enumeration, OS commands, or environment probing appears at those lines.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credentialed External Payment Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples obtain OAuth tokens and call PayPal payment endpoints with bearer authorization. This is normal for PayPal integration, but it requires secret storage, timeout handling, logging controls, and sandbox separation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples explicitly pass client credentials and bearer tokens to PayPal API requests. The pattern is expected but sensitive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Webhook-Like Input Drives Business Actions
dangerous_patterns.description: IPN request data controls payment, refund, and chargeback handlers. The sample includes PayPal verification and duplicate checks, but production implementations must also verify merchant, amount, currency, and order ownership.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code path is clear: request form data is verified then used to trigger financial state changes. The risk is business logic hardening, not malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Teams need reliable payment flows without missing PayPal security requirements. This skill guides checkout, subscriptions, IPN handling, refunds, and sandbox testing.
actual_capabilities: Explains PayPal Checkout and Smart Payment Buttons integration.
Shows server-side order creation, capture, and order lookup patterns.
Provides IPN verification and payment event handling guidance.
Covers subscription plan creation and subscriber approval flows.
Outlines refund creation, refund lookup, error handling, and sandbox tests.
limitations: Examples need production hardening before handling real payments.
It does not provide a complete deployable payment service.
It assumes users already have PayPal developer credentials.
It does not replace legal, tax, or compliance review.

use_cases.title: Add PayPal Checkout
use_cases.description: Implement a PayPal checkout path with client buttons and server-side order capture.

use_cases.title: Process Payment Notifications
use_cases.description: Design IPN handlers that verify PayPal events before updating orders or fulfillment.

use_cases.title: Manage Recurring Billing
use_cases.description: Plan PayPal subscription products, billing cycles, subscriber approval, and refund flows.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Checkout Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PayPal integration skill to outline a basic checkout flow for a one-time purchase. Include frontend, backend, and sandbox steps.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Order Capture Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PayPal integration skill to review my order creation and capture flow. Identify missing validation, error handling, and sandbox checks.

prompt_templates.title: Design Subscription Billing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PayPal integration skill to design a subscription billing flow. Include plan creation, subscriber approval, renewal handling, cancellations, and refunds.

prompt_templates.title: Harden IPN and Refund Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PayPal integration skill to audit an IPN and refund workflow. Focus on authenticity, idempotency, order ownership, amount checks, and operational logging.

output_examples.input: I need PayPal Checkout for a digital product.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan covering Smart Buttons, backend order capture, sandbox testing, and post-payment fulfillment checks.
A security-focused review listing verification steps, duplicate transaction handling, merchant validation, amount checks, and recommended failure responses.
A workflow summary for subscription plans, subscriber approval, renewal events, cancellation states, refund processing, and test coverage.

best_practices: Keep PayPal credentials in a secret manager or protected environment variables.
Verify every PayPal notification and make payment processing idempotent.
Use sandbox accounts and separate production configuration before launch.
anti_patterns: Do not trust client-side payment callbacks as proof of payment.
Do not process IPN data without checking merchant, amount, currency, and transaction identity.
Do not mix sandbox credentials, production endpoints, or live customer data.

faq.question: Does this skill process payments directly?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and sample patterns for building PayPal payment integrations.

faq.question: Can I use the examples in production as written?
faq.answer: No. Treat them as starting points and add validation, timeouts, monitoring, and compliance controls.

faq.question: Does it cover PayPal subscriptions?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes subscription plan creation, subscriber approval, and recurring billing concepts.

faq.question: Does it include IPN handling?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains IPN verification and event handling for completed payments, refunds, and reversals.

faq.question: What credentials do I need?
faq.answer: You need PayPal developer client credentials and separate sandbox or production configuration.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
